MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFFS YULONG ECO-MATERIALS LTD, and EV BIOLOGICS CORP.'S, FORMERLY KNOWN AS YULONG Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses By Ridgway Smith And For Monetary Sanctions.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling. Motion DENIED as untimely. CCP 2024.060 permits the parties to agree to extensions but provides that any such agreement "be confirmed in a writing that specifies the extended date." Thus, open-ended extensions are not permitted because they do not "specif[y] the extended date." While Attorney Glickfeld wrote on April 28, 2023, that "there is no deadline for you to file a motion" on further discovery responses (Nemesek Decl. Ex M/pg 103 of 108), that offer did not comply with the statute that requires a specified extended date. The fact that Defendant served new responses serves to reset the date for motions.

Off Calendar Apr-10-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: CROSS DEFENDANT EV BIOLOGIES CORP.'s MOTION TO STRIKE Amended CROSS COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

EV Biologics Corp.'s motion to strike portions of the third amended cross-complaint (TACC) is granted. The motion attacks two TACC passages with three arguments. (Not. 2:3-14; TACC 19:19-22, 21:9.)

First, defendant "cannot recover restitution on behalf of others" under his unfair competition law (UCL) claim because this case is not a class action. (Not. 2:9-12; Amalgamated Transit Union, Local 1756, AFL-CIO v. Sup. Ct. (2009) 46 Cal.4th 993, 1005 ["all unfair competition law actions seeking relief on behalf of others must be brought as class actions"].) Second, "attorney's fees are not recoverable" under a UCL claim. (Not. 2:11; Cel-Tech Communications, Inc. v. Los Angeles Cellular Telephone Co. (1999) 20 Cal.4th 163, 179.) Third, defendant says "we are not requesting injunctive relief with respect to the UCL." (Opp. 4:3; Not. 2:13.)

Order signed.
